page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.e-c1m2.2xlarge is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine instance belonging to the ecs.e server family. It is configured with 8 shared Intel Xeon vCPUs running at a clock speed of 2.5 GHz, backed by 8 physical cores with 1.0 thread per core. The instance features 16.0 GB of system memory, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB per core. It operates on a KVM hypervisor and includes CPU cache capacities of 32 KB L1d, 1.0 MB L2, and 33 MB L3. No local storage or GPU hardware is bundled with this instance type, and it delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks place this instance in the average tier across multiple categories. The stress-ng single-core CPU performance is measured at 1729.79, while the multi-core performance reaches 7072.24. In memory bandwidth testing, the instance achieves 570.8 GB/sec for cached small-block reads, 80.28 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads, and 49.91 GB/sec for large-block writes. PassMark benchmarks report a CPU score of 11140.77 and a database operations score of 4207.46. The instance demonstrates a static web serving throughput of 10.17 GB/sec and handles 9.25 million operations per second in Redis SET operations.

Given its shared CPU allocation and 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, the ecs.e-c1m2.2xlarge offers a cost-efficient profile for workloads that require multi-core processing without dedicated CPU pinning. The lack of local storage requires reliance on network-attached storage options. This instance is suitable for entry-level web serving, lightweight database operations, Redis caching, and general-purpose utility workloads that can tolerate shared CPU resources.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.e-c1m2.2xlarge is an x86_64 virtual server from the ecs.e family, featuring 8 shared Intel Xeon vCPUs at 2.5 GHz and 16.0 GB of RAM. Operating on a KVM hypervisor, this instance provides 2.0 GB of memory per core and lacks local storage or GPU accelerators. Benchmark results indicate average performance, with a PassMark CPU score of 11140.77 and a static web serving throughput of 10.17 GB/sec. The instance handles Redis SET operations at 9.25 million operations per second and achieves a multi-core stress-ng score of 7072.24. With its shared CPU allocation and baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps, this instance offers a cost-effective solution for non-critical, compute-oriented workloads. It is best suited for lightweight database operations, web serving, and caching applications.
